title: Animation Steps, after, Pause, Speed, and Cancellation · Program Brief: After Scheduling

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-after-scheduling — Schedule one future callback with after and retain its identifier and owning run generation.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-animation-after-cancel-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-animation-after-cancel-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_animation_after_cancel_4(scheduled_steps: int = 42, completed_steps: int = 42, pending_callbacks: int = 0, stale_callbacks: int = 0, pause_stable: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if scheduled_steps < 0 or completed_steps < 0:
        return False, completed_steps, "animation-count"
    if completed_steps != scheduled_steps:
        return False, completed_steps, "missing-step"
    if pending_callbacks > 0 or stale_callbacks > 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks + stale_callbacks, "callback-leak"
    if not pause_stable:
        return False, completed_steps, "pause-mutation"
    assert completed_steps == scheduled_steps and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, completed_steps, "cancellable-animation"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Schedule one future callback with after and retain its identifier and owning run generation.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the After Scheduling model · Theory After Scheduling, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, After Scheduling precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the After Scheduling model · Theory After Scheduling, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 8133 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to model bounded scheduled animation with pause, changed speed, cancellation, and stale-work rejection; all graph claims need an independent witness; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Schedule one future callback with after and retain its identifier and owning run generation.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the After Scheduling model · Theory After Scheduling, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: after(delay, callback) blocks until the callback completes.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-after-scheduling-8f9njg(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_after_scheduling_8f9njg\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?scheduled_steps < 0 or completed_steps < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?completed_steps != scheduled_steps)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pending_callbacks > 0 or stale_callbacks > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not pause_stable)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
